title: Israel unleashes unlimited firepower in Iran and Lebanon
subhead: The country launched an operation against Hezbollah in Lebanon on Monday, after the Iranian-allied armed group fired rockets at it. The Israeli military is now seriously engaged in a two-front war.

title: France's nuclear deterrence takes major step toward Europe
subhead: During Monday's speech at the Ile Longue naval base, Emmanuel Macron stressed cooperation with France's allies across Europe, unveiled a new doctrine of 'advanced deterrence' and announced an increase in the number of France's nuclear warheads.

title: Iran war reignites debate over France's role in the Middle East
subhead: With fears of escalation around Iran stirring debate just two weeks before the municipal elections, France's position in the region is under scrutiny by its political parties.

title: What does international law say about the Israeli-American attack on Iran and Tehran's retaliation?
subhead: Aggression, self-defense, preemptive strikes: The United States, Israel and Iran each justified their military actions, but legal experts question their legality.

title: France's Iranian artistic diaspora torn between anxiety and joy
subhead: Since the US-Israeli strikes on Iran, artists in the diaspora have found themselves dreaming of better days, even as they fear a rising death toll.

subhead: An influential voice in the progressive camp and former advisor to Senator Bernie Sanders, Matthew Duss harshly criticizes Donald Trump's actions following Saturday's strikes, stating that 'this war is unfortunately the culmination of decades of a militarist foreign policy.'

title: Photographer Robert Frank's love letter to Paris
subhead: During a stay in Paris in the autumn of 1949, Swiss photographer Robert Frank created an album of the French capital for his fiancée, Mary, who had remained in the United States. In around 80 freely arranged prints, he captured the city's streets, inhabitants and everyday objects. This notebook has now been published, revealing the emergence of a style that would later explode in 'The Americans,' the photographer's seminal work.

title: The 'great replacement' theory: 'A hateful, conspiratorial phrase' now in 'everyday language'
subhead: The expression, coined by far-right ideologue Renaud Camus in 2010, is based on a myth Hitler developed. Its drift into everyday language marks a turning point, according to Le Monde columnist Philippe Bernard.
